title: 3 ways Cade Cunningham’s absence could impact the Celtics
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Cade Cunningham of the Detroit Pistons is out with a collapsed lung, expected to be re-evaluated in two weeks.
Cunningham's absence could benefit the Boston Celtics, who are 3.5 games behind the Pistons with 13 regular-season games left.
Jaylen Brown's MVP chances could improve as Cunningham, fourth in MVP odds, may miss the rest of the regular season.

title: Readers say this is Massachusetts’ most stressful commute — and it’s not I-93 North
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Boston.com readers identify the Southeast Expressway/Rt. 93 Southbound near South Bay as Massachusetts' most stressful commute.
I-95/Route 128 in Waltham received 18% of votes, with frustrations around traffic density and unpredictability.
The Zakim Bridge/I-93 North, previously ranked as the worst, was named by only 13% of readers as the most stressful route.

title: A grave situation: Medway police seek return of 12-foot-tall skeleton’s head
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Medway Police seek a missing head from the 12-foot-tall skeleton 'Kevin Skellington' at Medway Recreation Center.
The skeleton has been a staple since 2023, regularly dressed in themed costumes for holidays at Choate Park.
Police are investigating the disappearance as larceny; security footage is being reviewed for leads.

title: Dropkick Murphys to play at ‘No Kings’ rally on Boston Common
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Dropkick Murphys to perform at 'No Kings' rally on Boston Common, March 28 from 2 to 4 p.m.
The 'No Kings Day' action includes coordinated protests nationwide, organized by ACLU and Indivisible Mass Coalition.
The rally protests against policies of Donald Trump's presidency, supported by bands like Dropkick Murphys.

title: Sold out show? Not if you book at this Boston hotel.
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Omni Boston Hotel at the Seaport offers an All Access Pass for sold-out concerts and events in 2026.
The All Access Pass includes accommodations, event tickets, transportation, and optional VIP experiences.
Packages range from $1,000 to $1,300, with access to events like Lady Gaga concerts and the World Cup.

title: Six from N.H. charged in ‘brazen’ Downtown Crossing assault and robbery
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Six New Hampshire residents charged with assault and robbery at Boston's Downtown Crossing station.
Victim, 19, was chased, beaten, and robbed of phone, ID, and debit cards.
Suspects identified using phone tracking and surveillance; court date set for May 19.

title: Review: ‘Project Hail Mary’ borders on miraculous
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Ryan Gosling stars in 'Project Hail Mary', a sci-fi adventure blending humor and suspense, directed by Phil Lord and Christopher Miller.
Adapted from Andy Weir's novel, the film features Ryan Gosling as a scientist tasked with solving Earth's extinction-level crisis.
'Project Hail Mary' excels in humor but is critiqued for its lengthy runtime and less compelling final act.

title: City Council calls for more transparency from Wu over transportation projects
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston City Council demands transparency from Mayor Wu over stalled transportation projects needing her personal approval.
Council President Liz Breadon and others cite nearly $200 million in potential jeopardy from delayed projects, including MBTA upgrades.
Hearing orders by Breadon and Durkan seek clarity on funding deadlines and call for updated city transportation goals.

title: ‘All the negativity that’s in this town sucks’: Revisiting Rick Pitino’s iconic Celtics rant
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Rick Pitino's Celtics coaching career ended with a 102-146 record over three-and-a-half seasons.
Pitino's March 1, 2000 rant criticized expectations for Celtic legends to rescue the struggling team.
The rant highlighted frustrations with the negative Boston sports atmosphere, reflected in the iconic soundbite.

title: Joe Mazzulla’s ‘tough love’ reminds Robert Parish of a particular Hall-of-Fame coach
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Robert Parish compares Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla's 'tough love' style to former coach Bill Fitch.
Parish praises Mazzulla's martial arts background and ability to manage players' roles effectively.
Parish highlights the Celtics' teamwork, confidence, and ability to succeed without star player Jayson Tatum.
